steps.title: Identify the standard form of a circle equation
steps.content: The standard form of a circle equation reveals the center and radius directly. If an equation matches this pattern, the center is at $(h,k)$ and the radius is $r$, with no additional work needed.

steps.title: Match the given equation to the standard form
steps.content:
The given equation is $$(x-13)^2 + (y-k)^2 = 64$$ Comparing directly:
  • $(x-13)$ means $h = 13$
  • $(y-k)$ means the $y$-coordinate is $k$
  • $64 = 8^2$, so $r = 8$
The center is at $(13, k)$ and the radius is $8$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
